data.title: Organigram to acquire Sanity Group GmbH, German cannabis company.
data.description: In Toronto, Organigram Global Inc., a major cannabis producer listed on NASDAQ and TSX as OGI, has signed a Purchase Agreement to acquire all shares of Sanity Group GmbH that it does not already own. The Acquisition will involve payment to Sanity shareholders, and upon completion, Organigram will take full ownership of the company.
Hungary FM: ‘Russia has not attacked any EU member state. Ukraine is defending itself. We do not owe Ukraine anything. Our security is guaranteed by NATO, not Ukraine.’

data.title: Feb 18 earnings: GLBE $327.89M revenue, FVRR $108.87M, WING $176.96M, TX $3.75B revenue, ADI $3.12B revenue.
data.description: Before the bell, various companies are set to report their quarterly earnings and revenue figures. Global E Online (NASDAQ: GLBE) is projected to report earnings of $0.36 per share on revenue of $327.89 million, Constellium (NYSE: CSTM) is expected to report earnings of $0.33 per share on revenue of $1.89 billion, and Fresh Del Monte Produce (NYSE: FDP) is likely to report earnings of $0.28 per share on revenue of $1.01 billion. Other companies like Fiverr Intl (NYSE: FVRR), Analog Devices (NASDAQ: ADI), and Moodys (NYSE: MCO) are also set to announce their figures. After the bell, TKO Group Holdings (NYSE: TKO) is expected to report earnings at $0.13 per share. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Camtek reports record Q4 results with revenues reaching $128.1M & full-year revenues hitting $496.1M in 2025; expects double-digit revenue growth in 2026. CEO optimistic about the AI market's strong demand. Q4 GAAP net income at $35.9M, non-GAAP at $40.7M. Full year GAAP net income at $50.7M, non-GAAP at $159.0M. Cash on hand as of Dec 31, 2025, reached $851.1M compared to $448.6M in 2024. Conference call scheduled for further updates.
data.description: Camtek Ltd. reported record revenues of $128.1 million for Q4 2025, a 9% increase YoY, and $496.1 million for the full year 2025, a 16% increase YoY. GAAP net income for Q4 was $35.9 million, up 9% YoY, and for the full year was $50.7 million (down 43% YoY). Non-GAAP net income for Q4 was $40.7 million, up 8% YoY, and for the full year was $159.0 million, up 15% YoY. Operating cash flow for 2025 was $142.6 million. Cash and equivalents were $851.1 million at the end of 2025. Management anticipates double-digit revenue growth in 2026, with Q1 revenues expected around $120 million and significant growth in the second half of the year. Camtek is positioned well in the AI market with strong demand, expecting continued growth. The company will host a conference call to discuss results and future outlook.

data.title: Alter Domus chosen by Bain Capital Credit to provide services for its $30bn global credit portfolios, including CLO and bank loan settlement services.
data.description: Alter Domus, a prominent global provider of tech-driven fund and corporate services for the alternative investment sector, has been chosen by Bain Capital Credit to administer CLO middle and back office services along with bank loan settlement services. This partnership will cater to Bain Capital Credit's worldwide liquid and structured credit portfolios exceeding $30 billion in value. Alter Domus will offer a wide range of middle and back-office solutions as part of this mandate.

data.title: Tigo Energy program sees 1,500 Green Glove installer engagements, enhancing quality standards.
data.description: A California-based company, Tigo Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: TYGO), known for its intelligent solar and energy software solutions, revealed that its expansive network of solar installers has participated in 1,500 engagements as part of the Green Glove service program. This program plays a crucial role in reinforcing the company's dedication to Total Quality Solar by enhancing the installer experience. For example, Brighthouse Solar of Central California has been a beneficiary of this initiative.

data.title: Survey by Harvard Business Review Analytic Services finds 94% of organizations exploring AI face readiness gap.
data.description: In Redwood Shores, California, Reltio, a context intelligence platform, unveiled a new pulse survey conducted by Harvard Business Review Analytic Services. Titled "Unlocking the Data Advantage in the Age of Intelligence," the survey, supported by Reltio, reveals that 94% of organizations are either investigating or putting into action AI technologies. However, a significant gap persists between companies' ambitions for enterprise AI and their preparedness in terms of data utilization.

data.title: Netcracker highlights agentic AI for telcos at MWC 2026 in Barcelona. Showcasing real-world use cases.
data.description: WALTHAM, Mass. – In an upcoming event in Barcelona, Netcracker is set to present a state-of-the-art AI solution tailored for telecommunication companies. The demonstration will feature practical applications of this advanced technology during the MWC 2026 conference.

data.title: Zoho Corp celebrates 30th anniversary, serving 1M+ customers &150M+ users globally, with 32% YoY customer growth.
data.description: In Austin, Texas, Zoho Corporation, a global tech company, celebrated its 30th anniversary by revealing two important accomplishments. The company, comprised of Zoho, ManageEngine, Qntrl, and TrainerCentral, has now surpassed one million paying customers and over 150 million users worldwide. This announcement comes after notable yearly increases of 32% in customers and 20% in revenue in 2025. The corporation expresses gratitude for achieving these milestones.

data.title: Ada launches first Unified Reasoning Engine for AI agents, revolutionizing customer experience with a single intelligence layer.
data.description: In Toronto, Ada, a leading AI-native customer experience company renowned for the Agentic Customer Experience Operating Model, revealed its latest innovation - the Reasoning Engine™. This patented advancement revolutionizes the functionality of AI agents on a large scale within enterprises, facilitating quicker and more intricate task automation for extended periods. The unique feature of this development is the utilization of a singular, cohesive intelligence to empower AI agents, effectively acting as a centralized "brain".

data.title: Bayer proposes $7.25B settlement for Roundup cancer claims in US, acquired Monsanto for over $60B in 2018.
data.description: Bayer AG has put forth a $7.25 billion settlement offer to settle numerous U.S. lawsuits alleging that its weedkiller Roundup causes cancer. Originally developed by Monsanto, which Bayer acquired for over $60 billion in 2018, the settlement aims to provide closure amid ongoing litigation challenges. CEO Bill Anderson stated that the settlement would diminish potential risks related to a future Supreme Court decision, providing clarity for both Bayer and affected individuals seeking compensation. Under the agreement, Monsanto will finance the settlement through limited annual payments spread out over a maximum of 21 years, totaling up to $7.25 billion. Additionally, Monsanto will resolve other Roundup (glyphosate) cases under undisclosed terms. The proposed settlement was submitted in Missouri's St. Louis Circuit Court.

data.title: Global E Online (NASDAQ: GLBE) Q4 earnings: Missed estimated EPS by -2.78% at $0.35 vs $0.36. Revenue increased by $73.75M.
data.description: Global E Online, traded as GLBE on NASDAQ, released its Q4 earnings report on February 18, 2026, at 06:02 AM. The company fell short of anticipated earnings by -2.78%, with an EPS of $0.35 compared to the estimated $0.36. Revenue showed a significant increase of $73.75 million from the previous year's Q4. This performance indicates a consistent trend of missing earnings expectations. For more details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Constellium (CSTM) Q4 report: EPS beat by 142.42% at $0.8 vs $0.33 estimate. Revenue up $480 million.
data.description: Constellium (NYSE: CSTM) unveiled its Q4 earnings on February 18, 2026, at 06:00 AM. The report exceeded expectations with an EPS of $0.8, surpassing the estimated $0.33 by 142.42%. Revenue showed significant growth, increasing by $480.00 million compared to the previous year. This latest announcement highlights the company's robust financial performance. Last quarter's earnings also outperformed projections. More details are accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: California initiates DFAL enforcement for crypto licensing. Firms must obtain licenses by mid-2026 to operate in the state.
data.description: By mid-2026, cryptocurrency companies operating in California must obtain DFAL licenses or submit license applications. This mandate applies to all firms servicing the state's residents. Failure to comply could result in penalties or legal action. It is essential for these businesses to meet this requirement to ensure they are operating within the legal framework. This regulation aims to enhance consumer protection and regulatory oversight within the cryptocurrency industry. Therefore, companies must act promptly to adhere to these guidelines and avoid any disruptions to their operations.

data.title: Wells Fargo predicts $150B inflow into Bitcoin and risk assets from surge in retail risk-taking post higher US tax refunds.
data.description: According to a strategist from Wells Fargo, the possibility of larger US tax refunds may lead to increased risk-taking in the retail sector by the end of March. This could result in a flow of new funds into assets like Bitcoin and momentum stocks.

data.title: KBR and Petro Rabigh sign 10-year maintenance contract for Saudi Arabia polymer plants.
data.description: In Houston on February 18, 2026, KBR (NYSE: KBR) and Rabigh Refining & Petrochemical Company (Petro Rabigh) revealed a significant collaboration - a 10-year general maintenance services agreement, with the potential for a two-year extension. This deal focuses on maintaining the operations of Petro Rabigh Polymer I and Polymer II plants based in Rabigh, Saudi Arabia.
